title: datura stramonium subsp. tatula
intro: Datura stramonium subsp. tatula, also known as the devil's trumpet or Jamestown weed, is a plant species within the nightshade family that is native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, primarily for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and anesthetic effects. The health benefits of Datura stramonium subsp. tatula include its ability to relieve muscle spasms, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Datura stramonium subsp. tatula is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, where it is typically combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
